text: When i first switched to t mobile it was good. But now my phone drops calls all the time. I dont get the calls that r coming in.a lot of places i dont even have service. What happen.  Can't dial out. It's not worth the trouble for the saving i got for switching from verizon

Went to a store to transfer service my phone and my wife's from Verizon.  An hour and half later, the three reps still couldn't figure out how to port our numbers.  We decided to leave; our service never left Verizon, not even for a second.  T-Mobile decided to not only charge me activation fees for something that was never activated, they set me up on a monthly auto-bill!  Imagine my surprise when I saw the $150 charge.  The store wouldn't refund my money, the online rep wouldn't refund my money, and two phone reps hung up on me!  Finally got an online rep to tell me that the only way to get a refund is to wait for some special department to call me, which could take 5-7 business days!  It's been 14, still no call!  -Jeremy Raczkowski

I barely ever have service you can't ever watch videos it takes forever and a day to load something when I go to call someone I have to hang up and call again about ten times just to get it to actually ring and even when I finally get through the service is such crap that it breaks in and out I have done a factory data reset about ten times and still has not fixed anything at all I really hate this service and once my bill is paid I will be moving to a better more reliable carrier the only reason they got one star is because there is no option for 0 stars

I have been trying to get my phone unlocked and they literally had me on hold for one hour. I just hung up tried to go to the T-Mobile store in town to see if they could help and still nothing. I tried calling back again and there I was waiting even longer. I’ve spent my whole morning trying to get my phone unlocked to switch my service and I didn’t get it done and now I have to go to work. I’m extremely frustrated.

Horrendous customer service. My best friend recently died & I am dealing with her final finances through probate court.  It has taken 3 times & nearly 2 hours of my time to get them to understand the situation, including a visit to a store. Every time I contact them I get a different answer & transferred several times   They are trying to get me to pay for service months after she died. GO WITH ANOTHER MOBILE COMPANY.  I don't have TMobile & never will.
They suck. They lie and after hours on the phone they cannot get my bill right. Don’t use

I used to like T-Mobile till I tried to add a digits line with them. Weeks later my second line is still not working. They have already changed the number twice (they charged me $16 and some change each time). I have called in off and on over the last 3 weeks and the reps say their is an IT ticket in and they make me go through the same steps over and over. Clearly something is wrong on your end! Stop making me sign in and sign out. It's not working!
I'm about to lose it! I've wasted hours of my life with them and already ordered and received my new business cards with the "new" non-working number.
Ideally, I would like the number to finally work. I would like a credit for the two line changes ($16 x 2) and I would like to be reimbursed with a credit for the hours of my wasted time. I charge $40 an hour (I estimate about 4 hours on the phone with T-Mobile).
 If you plan on changing the number again, I need to be reimbursed for the business cards too.
I will change companies soon, if this is not addressed. Thank you.

I haven’t had service worth a darn in the 4 months I’ve been with T-Mobile.  Have to drive 25 miles east to call anyone. Cannot make or receive a call on my front porch.  
They promised me the moon and gave NO promotions. No BOGO as promised , no buy out as promised and most of all NO service!  Buying my way out and not receiving a thing. Phones cannot be unlocked so they will be worthless. Save yourself and run from T-Mobile.

I switched to T-mobile from Verizon about a year ago because my bill was too expensive. They say you can bring your phone from a previous carrier but what they don't tell you is you have to manually download your own software updates. When i try to download the software I get an error message that I don't have enough space on my laptop which is not true. My phone hasn't had an update since last September. On top of that my service is horrible when I am at home in certain parts of my house or out in certain areas. Most times my incoming calls go straight to voicemail and if someone leaves a message it comes through like 20 minutes later. Now I am about to switch again smh.

I've been troubleshooting my phone for 2 days I'm very unsatisfied I wouldn't do two different stores they were rude to me then I called and talked to a manager who was all so rude to me I'm going to pay to get out of this contract beware of TMobile they will rip you off can't even contact my husband won't let me call or text

I had chosen T-Mobile a little over a year ago and I have to admit the experience was less than pleasant. To start the monthly rate was more than promised for example a monthly bill of over $140 for two cell phones. 
Secondly, once I had to move abroad I canceled the contract and paid the remainder of the phones. So far, I was okay. However, once paid both phones were still locked which made the phones useless abroad. To unlock the phones I had to call T-Mobile (International call) to just be transferred over and over again. Since, the lines were also canceled they couldn't retrace the phones and thus, couldn't unlock them. 
Thirdly, two months later I received a bill of (auto-billing $149) I was outraged by this because I thought I had canceled the contract and yet I'm still getting charged... I then, chose to call them once again, to stay on an international phone call for over 25 minutes to hear that since I don't have the "Password" they couldn't do anything... After multiple of attempts asking for managers and supervisors, getting passed on from a line to another, they asked that I give them my current phone number so that they could call back. I have yet to hear from them. 

Overall: Customer service 1* out of 5, Billing 1* out of 5, Problem solving 1* out of 5, overall option of T-Mobile: Disappointing, unreliable and not worth the money, time and frustration... 

Sincerely.

Had problems from the second I started with this worthless company. Lied to at the store, haven't been able to access my account since I started, couldn't switch from prepaid to post pay, spent 1hr 50 mins being put on hold and transferred at least 9 times, got my account double charged, app hasn't worked once for me, randomly has message blocking active and the method they give you too fix it DOESN'T EXIST! I'm not just canceling t-mobile, I'm talking to a lawyer!

Scammers.  Customer servers over the phone they tells you wrong information misleading you and after that you find charges to your account. I bought two phones on the promo buy one get one free after 90 days they are telling me that my account is not qualified for free phone ..and no rebates for me it is $720 phone.. they are scammers.. DON'T BUY FROM T MOBILE NOTHING THEY ARE DISHONEST.  My account is 847 890 1376

I am very dissatisfied with customer service by phone. So tired of being told different stories regarding a product I returned to store and credit for it. Continued to be charge d for something I returned to the store and do not have. So very frustrated. I'm feeling like going back to my other carrier. You sold me a phone that your employee told me over heats and looses charge fast along with running slow.. 3 months after I purchased it. Now I am worried just trying to upsell me again. Then being charged for a returned product telling me I could use that credit towards that returned item to get it off of being charged every month.. Buyer beware when signing on!!!

This I-Phone 7 plus Can’t Share Anything Social media At all .. Took It to the store they couldn’t figure it out. Called Tech Support they couldn’t figure it out. I have this Jump upgrade yet they can’t Waive the Down payment for the Note 9 that I want. This Current phone isn’t working properly. So No i Dont want the same phone, No Lesser phone. It should be Waived for that Phone. So I’m going leave this Everywhere I can think of Knowing they hate Bad Reviews. Here you Go T-Mobile . . Terrible.

They suck. Limited service, but they will tell you different. Cancelled within 3 days because they misrepresented service area, well winthin buyers remorse period of 30 days and they continued to bill me. Customer
Service unhelpful, had to cancel credit card to avoid further charges. And then without warning they turned us over to a collection agency. This was the most stressful company that I ever had the displeasure of dealing with.

Garbage problem after problem don’t waste your time stick with the proper phone carrier Verizon wireless.  these people they can’t even process a  payment waste of  time with these people total disgrace.   I’m switching back to Verizon

edits.text: Worthless carrier, terrible service  rip off marketing company.  Stay with #1 Verizon.  They offer the same with number one coverage by far working anywhere  in the United States unlike t mobile

text: I was in Europe when I got a notice from my carrier, Consumer Cellular, that I was out of roaming minutes.  When I tried to call them, it went straight to T-Mobile, which they contract with as the international provider.  After waiting many minutes for a representative, they said I needed to call Consumer Cellular.  I had tried to call again, and again it went to T-Mobile.  Another representative gave me another number for CC.  No luck.  Another representative said they could not patch me through to CC and could not help me get roaming and would not put me through to a supervisor.  I was stuck with a broken leg and no phone service.  Thank goodness for WhatsApp which allowed me to call my daughters.  You can expect sympathy but no action from the reps.

I called to TMoible three times and each time talked to a different person.  Each person gave me different answers.  They would not give a direct answer.  When I finally nailed down answers, my brother went in to get the plan we discussed.  A fourth person said that plan did not exist.  The plan is on their web site and the store said they have that plan... They began offering other plans and kept coming back with additional costs.   My brother finally walked out on them.   How can there be so many employees who give out false information?  Are they trained this to be this way?

I've had T-Mobile service for over 19 years (they were Powertel back then) and have seen the company grow into the best in America (my opinion). At one time I had two phones; one with T-Mobile and one with Verizon. The T-Mobile coverage was as good as or better than Verizon in at least 95% of the places I travel. However, my Verizon bill was over $800 more per year. 
One IMPORTANT piece of advice: Get a quality phone. When I upgraded my phone I found my coverage improved dramatically.

I switched to T-Mobile in May. Wrong decision. I have had nothing but issues with my new phones since day one. The service is ok not bad but the phones are crap and weird stuff keeps happening. Glitches here and there. The worst was 8 days after on of the phones ( 3 lines total) screen just stopped working. You could still hear all the alerts but black screen. I contacted a rep and they told me no problem they will mail me a new phone and I can mail them back the damaged phone. 2 weeks later I get a bill and naturally I call to inquire and I have talked to 3 different customer service representatives and none of them can help me. I was told there was a crack in the screen which wasn't there when I sent it originally. They said that because there is no damage to the box I have to pay for a new phone. I'm sorry but we all know how delicate cells are. You can drop a box and what's inside can shatter with no damage to the box. I think it's pretty horrible that after 8 days I have to buy another phone cus there crappy phone stopped working and it's my word against there's about a crack. Complete and utter bullcrap!!

So T-Mobile has a promotion for $99 iPhone 6 yet when I went into the store they told me I have to give up 200 some plus dollars I found  that advertising is misleading  and because of that at the end of my billing cycle I'm switching to Verizon and I'm no longer been a T-Mobile customer of two years I'm done. It's a shame that T-Mobile have to stoop to Petty promotional lies to get people in that store and I was happy with TMobile up until this point

I had an account with Orange - which was bought by T mobile. I later upgraded my account and found that T mobile kept all of the credit I had stored during my contract - this was over 600 euros. I have tried many times to get some sort of explanation, which they refuse to discuss. In my opinion they are certainly thieves - 600 euros is a lot of money to keep, especially when I was only upgrading the contract. They gave me no warning that they would be keeping this amount otherwise I would never have upgraded.- I even messaged the top brass at T mobile, but hey no reply...

edits.text: I was a customer for 15 years+ and would not recommend  them to my worst enemy. When I upgraded my contract, they kept nearly 600 euros that I had in credit. No mention beforehand that they would be keeping all of my credit. Cancelling the contract was also a big problem, the shop advised me I just needed to give one months notice, when I called to cancel, yes there were problems. Just a machine to squeeze you for as much as possible.

text: Worst customer service ever! They offer good promotions but the bill never comes as they said so. Everytime you call a customer service they give you different answers and after 2 yrs of service , they come up saying you are not eligible to the promotion they offered in the first place. TMOBILE sucks. Waste of time and money.

I can't get a hold of my fiancee for over 3 weeks now. The signal is dead. No connection. T mobile cant give me any resolution. Very unhappy with this situation. Cant describe how frustated I am. This is the worst experience ever.

I had called T-Mobile to try to figure out ways to lower my monthly bills. At the time I had a Simple Choice plan (2 for 100$) plus the "Two lines on us" plan. After looking online I found the T-Mobile One Military offers my 4lines for 100$ which is exactly what I was paying. The sell is the taxes and fees included. All good so far, but then I explained that I'm leasing the Galaxy S9+ (Jump-on-demand), but at 40-50$ a month, it's cheaper for me to just use another cheaper phone. The agent on the phone, after 20minutes of research, came back to say I could take my phone to a T-Mobile retail store and return it at no fee other than end of that month billing. She even stated that she copied and pasted the instructions into my account. Awesome right? So based on this news, I bought a phone on Amazon for about 350, much cheaper than the balance left on my S9+ and it does everything the Galaxy does. So I go to the King of Prussia retail store, but they tell me that while they will take the S9+ back from me, I owe 500+ dollars to pay off the phone. I explained to check my account, the agent on the phone notated that I did not owe anything. They said there's nothing they can do and told me to call back. So I went out and called T-Mobile. After 20monutes of the new agent looking into my claim, she came back and said that she'll send an email to the original agent that gave me inaccurate information and will email their manager. Then she specifically tells me to expect a phone call on Tuesday 7/24 from either that agent or that agents manager....no phone call.

So I've been liked to about being able to return my phone, out 350+$ due to basing my purchase on that information, wasted time and gas to go to a retail store, and then waited all day for a phone call that never came through while still holding onto a phone I don't want and a bill I shouldn't have to pay.

I've had the worst experience with T-Mobile. They had the promotion going on if you switch to them they'd pay off your phones from another company and my husband and I did so. Well, we were told to bring our bill from said company, did so, and the girls lost my bill and never even contacted me to say so. We kept receiving bills from the company and T-Mobile has done nothing to help us. We keep calling and calling and all they keep saying is "We need the bill". I understand that but  we gave them the bill and they couldn't handle the responsibility. I've had the worst experience with T-Mobile and will be leaving.

DO NOT GET THIS SERVICE THE COUSTMER SERVICE IT THE WORAT I HAVE EVER ENCOUNTERED. A TEAM LEAD TRIED TELLING ME THAT SHE IS THE HIGHEST PERSON I CAN TALK TO WHICH IS A LIR THEN SHE TOLD ME I CAN WROTE A LETTER TO TBERE LEGAL DEPARTMENT NOT TO MENTION THE FIRAT REP HING UP ON ME AFYER 35 MIN WAIT TIME THEY DO NOT VALUE THERE CUSTOMERS WHAT SO EVER THEY ARE NO HELP AND TREAT YOU LOKE YOUR DUMB IM DISGUSTED WOTH THIS COMPANY TRAC PHONE WOULD PROBALY TREAT YOU BETTER

AWFUL cell service in Northwest Indiana. The signal is so inconsistent by the general Southlake Mall area. I'll have 3-4 bars on 4G, yet it hardly functions at all. Some days I get a lovely 2kb/sec. Switching from WiFi data to cell towers is incredibly painful and it almost always causes total data disconnection. Most of the time I have to force airplane mode to get the connection with the tower to reset and function even though my phone reports the connection has been there the whole time. I never had this trouble with Sprint. Significantly worse network quality here.

2 stars for the T-mobile manager that helped us get our phones, he was great and knew everything.

Using a Galaxy S9+.

worst company ever, they charged me $85.00 for 2 days . I went back to Verizon . Very bad signal and bad customer service representative. They lie to me about the charges and they said I don't owe any money. I ended up fighting with collection agency. Stay away from this company, better choose a prepaid phone with cheaper service.

T-mobile sent me two boxs with labels to send two phones back over a year ago. Phones were sent back and now i get a call from collections saying i owe 900 dollars. T-mobile claims to have lost phones in warehouse and there unable to find tracking numbers on the labels they pay for. This is a joke, they get away with this all the time from what im reading. There making a fortune screwing hardworking ppl. My service now is metro, tmobile towers but no bs to deal with.

Awful. Never give them your banking info for automatic payments if your bank doesn't catch it they will take double payments, customer service is even worse, they fight you even when they know they are wrong they can't bill correctly to save there lives, and they coincidentally loose notes in your file and voice recordings to prove exactly what your saying. The old flip phones that are prepaid is a more viable option and a lot less headache. Save yourself time, money, trouble, and a big hassle. Avoid T-Mobile at all cost.

Tmobile is a horrible company, they will overcharge you after they lie to you about being on a long distance plan. Seems like they truly don't care about military despite their ads. Back in 2011 I was deployed and was told that I was on a plan that would greatly reduce charges if I called back home to talk to my wife or mother. Instead I came home to a $2800 bill for 3 phone calls. I was told that there was no record of signing up for the plan and that there was nothing they could do. I called about a month later and they said they would reverse the charges, but they sent it to collections instead. So they will lie to you and tell you what you want to hear but do the opposite. Then they have the nerve to say they support the troops....

Switched over from Verizon to T-Mobile a month ago. From day one we were experiencing dropped calls, lack of service, slow internet, and numerous areas without access. Initially we were told to wait 24hrs for their all their cell sites to be updated with our account. 48hrs later we were told to give it 72hrs. Several days after that we left for an international trip in which the problems continued. Upon returning we got on the phone again and went through several reset and troubleshooting procedures, which did not alleviate any of the issues. I tried canceling the service, but was asked to give them an opportunity to troubleshoot on their end. I inquired about the buyers remorse, since it was going to run out in a couple of days and was told not to worry, since we were dealing with open issues. A day later I called again and was told to acquire a cell signal extender as a possible fix to the problem we were experiencing at our home, again I asked about the buyers remorse period and was told not to worry; that if the extender did not fix the problem we would still be able to return their equipment for a full refund. Upon trying the extender, the problem persisted, so I called to cancel our service; at which time I was told that now we were a few days past the allowed return period. I escalated it to management and asked them to review the computer records and they confirmed all our calls and open issues, however as much as they would like to refund our money "if it was up to them", they were not able to, since their policy is "black and white". I cannot begin to express our extreme disappointment with T-Mobile as a cell service provider and their what I can only describe as unethical business practices.

I opened an account with T-Mobile in April, 2017. I tried to use their service for a few days, but the reception was horrible at the place I work and at my house. I returned the phone, paid the restocking fee and asked them to cancel my account. They said they took care of it, but I notice in August I had been billed two $100 payments. I called them and told them their error. They said they would refund my money and close my account. They refunded me $100 and still owe me $100 and only remove my credit card from the account, they did not close it. Since then I have received a bill saying that I owed them more money, a letter from one collection agency and then a second collection agency. After receiving each of these, I called T-Mobile to get this taken care of and ask for my $100 dollars. Each time, I wasted four or five hours being passed from agent to agent until one finally lied to me and said it was taken care, only to find out it was not.
Scam they just take you money for a trash service not happy

I am a new customer of tmobile, and this is the worst mistake of my life. If i could have given 0 stars i would have. I opened a business account with them and they confirmed that it would work in the area i lived and worked in. Now, i have the worst service i have ever had and i can't receive or make calls from where i work or live. Even when I'm at home on wifi, i still can't make calls like they said i could. I can't wait to get out of this service, I'm even thinking about just dropping them and letting them goon my credit. That's how  mad i am. Then when we tell them all the bad problems we are having, they tell us how we can spend more money with them to fix it. I am very disappointed in tmobile and i will make sure none of my friends make the same mistake.

I have not been a  customer of Tmobile for two years and they sent me a bill for 15 dollars and can't tell me why, but told me they sent me to collections (after I have been on the phone, mostly on hold with them for 45 minutes). I paid my bill every month on time every month when I was with them for like seven or nine years and left because of their lack luster coverage. This happened a year ago because of a computer error on tmobile but they weren't stupid enough to send me to collections over their mistake.

Since I signed up with T-Mobile it has been the worst experience. I called in because I wanted to have an international data plan for me and my partners and T-Mobile signed me up for a prepaid plan (where the international data feature is not possible to have with) but ensured me that I can have international data with it so  we had to find out that nothing works while my partners were in Europe where we needed to stay in contact with each other. Then they said there is a way to get them transferred to the enrolled plan (that I was asking for from the beginning on) and to keep the same SIM cards so I went to the T-Mobile store spent there 4 hours no lie. The guy that was working on my case ensured me that everything works now and the same SIM cards can be used now with the international data but it’s gonna take up to 24 hours to get activated. The next day NOTHING WORKS ANYMORE not even my phone in the US so I walked into a T-Mobile store again and they said “Oh the guy scanned new SIM cards so the old SIM cards don’t work anymore” and gave me new SIM cards that I had to pay for again (that’s just absurd). Finally it worked 2 months!!!!!! Later! But they still wanted me to pay for the 2 months for the service that I never wanted, for all the phone calls I had to make for hours and hours, for the visits in the store for hours and hours to get what I wanted from the beginning on. That’s just insane! Now finally everyone has working cards with international data but they are randomly booking additional services to my account so that I’m paying around 40$ more each month. They’ve been doing that for over 6 months now and I’m calling in every month to get those additional features removed since I never wanted to have them and they would always refund the charges. Now they assured me for the 10th time that it’s fixed but when I asked for a compensation or something for all the stress they’ve put me through to actually get what I want and only what I wanted they said: “we credited your account every month so you won’t get any more credits” I said yes but you only gave me the money back that I was paying for services I never wanted but I never got a compensation or something else. All in all I regret it every day that I signed up with them. The people that are working for T-Mobile are not trained right/good enough. They don’t know what they’re doing and are unfriendly on top of it. Don’t make the same mistake as I did buying service from them.

First they let someone walk into a store and buy a bunch of new IPhones on my account, no need to check ID or get my verification code, that makes sense. Since then my bill has been wrong every time and now the app wont work on my phone and they will only let me make payments over the phone. I have been a customer for more then 5 years with zero late payments and they are making it so I have to do extra steps to pay my bill. I have wasted hours of my life on hold and waiting for them to resolve issues only to be told there is nothing they can do, or that it is fixed and it is not. Avoid at all costs, not worth the headache.

I really wish I didn't have to put any stars for using T-Mobile. They deserve no props what so ever. When a person walks into a place that offers prepaid accounts and then quits the service because they aren't happy with the customer service, then they send you to collections... This is clearly fraud. Then when you call them to dispute the matter they call you a liar....? T mobile need to be put out of service.

After being a T-Mobile customer for over 15 years (I was with power-tell) we switched to another provider and T-Mobile contacted us about a new better rate plan if we would come back. We came back for one month, paid for that month and they could not get the new phones I had already purchased to work with their system. So we switched back to the other provider. And the billing department keeps trying to billing us for another month. I have spoken to ever customer no service that they have in I don't know how many countries and they can not get it fixed. I give up, don't bother to try them.

I've been with tmobile since they started a lot of years. the past year and a half has been unreal trying to deal with customer service over a tablet that I purchased at a tm store 2 months ago they told me the tablet that we had was broken and we needed a new one.didn't have one in the store so we put money down to have it shipped to us. In the meantime our tablet started working they put a new Sim card in the tablet and said no see it still doesn't work apparently all it needed was a Sim card the store didn't allow enough time for it to reconnect to the network so we cancelled the new tablet right away over 2 months later and 4 phone calls to customer service no refund on my down payment it's like they don't even have a record of my previous inquiries to customer service I'm also finding out about hidden fees they don't tell you about because they don't want you to know. I have to call my bank now to dispute that transaction on Monday morning. I'm switching to att asap especially since they were going to cut my phone off for not paying the whole bill I payed what it said to pay on my bill but they added 8 dollar calling customer service fee that I didn't even know about tmobile used to be the best now THEIR THE  WORST IM GONE

Horrible. I have been with Tmobile for close to 15 years. Unfortunately, I made the mistake to give them my account information for auto pay. After I had to switch service because their coverage in my area is just horrible(couldn't even make a phone call), they keep on putting charges on my account without warning or authorization. When I called them to inform them about the situation, they told me they would refund me a 100 dollar charge. They never did. So, I called them back to find out that they decided not to refund me the money. Thanks, Mobile! Keep the 100 dollars. I payed you guys close to 20 000 dollars over the last 15 years. That 100 won't kill me. Looking forward to giving my money to ATT for the next 25. Hope they care about their customers more so then you to! Goodbye!

Since T-Mobile merged with AT&T, the service has gone way down. Tried to unlock my phone and the lady that helped me shut down my phone and cleared my account. All customer service phone calls are routed to India so there's definitely confusion when asking for a simple task as unlocking a phone. This person not only cleared my account, she completely messed up my account so bad, now it's under a person named Jose. Say what!!!!????. I had to pay $100 to reconnect my phone trying to get my old number back now. It's been the worst experience ever!!!! Stay clear away from AT&T and T-Mobile!!!! The only reason I gave them two stars is due a Jesus who did his best help out.
I’m posting this on every social media that I can find till they get it right.

I have had T-mobile for about 10 years now and have lived in my current place for 8 years. I have been fighting with them for about 4 years now about my signal, being it is slowly diminishing. They told me it was everything but the tower. My phone worked fine where ever I went away from my house. My social media does not load, my calls drop with me moving my phone a matter of inches, if I walk from one room to another my call drops. People have told me I sound like a robot or I am under water. I have had 8 service tickets in the last 3 years and still nothing has been resolved. My phone actually works better in 4G than 4G LTE. After contacting the CEO through Facebook I got a call from corporate office and my last ticket was put in and I was told everything would be resolved by March of 2018 and it is now June of 2018 and my ticket was closed before Jan of 2018. Nothing was done after being told the tower does not have a signal that points in the direction of my house and the part was ordered and would be put up. The last woman I talked to told me there was nothing they could do because they have done everything they can do on their end (but fix the tower).  My next step is leaving T-mobile.

Paid $75.00/month for unlimited data and only got 200 megabytes because T-Mobile has no cell towers in this area. Called and rep said he would switch me to their $25.00/month plan with no data. That was late May. On June 9th I switched service to AT&T. On June 19th T-Mobile bills me another $75.00 (takes it out of my account). Call and they say they couldn't switch during the billing cycle. Yet they have charged me $75.00 for the UPCOMING billing cycle!

So T-Mobile has charged me $150.00 for 2 months of "Unlimted" data service and I've gotten a total of 200 megabytes of data! I WILL NEVER GO BACK TO T-MOBILE!!!!!!

I ordered a sim card via the phone and it was mailed to my address a few days later. I was charged $60.82. When I tried activating my new sim card, it was not working. I have spent the last two days on the phone with customer service trying to get this sim card issue resolved. I keep being told that the sim card is reserved and keep being put on hold and transferred. I then went to a T-mobile store and they also couldn't activate the sim card, so they put in a tech-ticket and told me to try again in 24 hours. I waited 24 hours and the sim card is still not working. I have called the sales department to try and get a refund and so far have been transferred four times and put on hold for over 40 minutes. The issue still has not been fixed. I am extremely fed up with T-mobile and do not recommend it to anyone. I will be filing a complaint.
The gentleman akeem who helped us was very calm polite and knowledgeable about all the devices great customer service

Been a costomer of t mobil for over 10 years and when i want to add a line they give me such a hard time “trying to protect my identity” 
Would not process my payment and was told to go in person to the store to also pay $25 verses $4 over the phone! Would not give me and other option. Unacceptable horribe customer service!

Greetings:
1). I just recently sent in a complaint to T-Mobile about an outstanding matter. I kept good records and sent that in to their corporate offices on a Thursday May 17, 2018. 
2). On Friday, May 18, 2018 by  I received a reply to my complaint that a member of their executive support team would contact me right away. 
3). On Saturday May 19, 2018 I received a call from a member of their corporate executive team with an apology from corporate that they had looked into my issue and had seen that the information which I provided was in deed accurate. That they had alerted the collections company and told them to reverse any actions which may have taken against me; they also informed me that they had contacted the credit reporting agency to have if any  negative posted comment about my account removed immediately from their records as it relates to this issue.
4).  The member of the corporate executive support team also informed me that if I should observed any further issues with this matter, that I should contact them as soon as possible and they would follow up on the issue.
5). This came as a shock to me for I have been with T-Mobil for over 12 years and never rally expected any form of reply from them – to just one customer in the thousands of users that they have? But this demonstrated to me and was told at the same time by the corporate executive support team person who had contacted me, that the  office of the President and CEO, takes their customers care and satisfaction very serious and that their first order of business..is their customers.
6). So for those who may be experiencing issues with their account and have not been able to have  them settled, despite all the efforts you are making,  if you keep good and accurate records of the dates and times and the person you have spoken to and what the outcomes were on each call, when you would have reached the limit, send an email or letter to their corporate offices in Bellevue, WA. 98006, and send in a detail explanation, just the facts, and leave out any abusive or insulting words, and request an investigation into your outstanding case.
7).  I am sure that when your complaint is received, they will respond to it without delay. It is to their advantage to keep their customers satisfied and if we can send to them issues that we experience, it well help them to provide training and perhaps even allow their technicians more access to prevent fewer complaints from  matters that could be handled at their own level.
8). I have not been asked to make this comment by anyone. I am doing so on my on free will, with no promised of any kind of reward from T-Mobile.

Kind Regards – CK

Once the new store in Rome NY opened I  switched from verizon over to tmobile because I was initially told it would be much cheaper. However once I received my bill it was more than Verizon! (After quoting me a different price) so to apologize they offered 2 credits on future bills. Which, the second one promised to me I didn't receive. I also got a buy one get one phone deal (great deal right) and was supposed to get an 800 dollar rebate card. Which I could then use to pay off my old verizon line. Well don't hold your breath on these rebate promises if you are switching to T-mobile. Its been over 3 months and I still have not received it. Even though I was told it would only take 15-30 days. They also told me they resubmitted it twice because they did it "incorrectly" twice. But did not offer to compensate for this mistake in any shape or form. So I have been paying for two seperate phone bills every month waiting on T-mobile. It has been by far the worst customer service and care I have received when I'm paying MORE than what I was with Verizon. They also tried to apply screen protectors twice and failed each time. Now both phones dont have screen protectors because apparently an $80 protector can't stay on. Not to mention the terribly spotty service. And in some cases I have to turn my whole phone off to "refresh" my service so to speak. It is a hassle and there should be no need to do such a thing. I wish I never switched.

I had been with Verizon(over 4 years), ATT(3 plus years), Metro PCS and Boost mobile and I'm now a happy customer of T Mobile. I don't usually write reviews but I have to say something about the customer service I've received from T Mobile because I see so much bad press about them. But I have nothing bad to say after over a year with them. I get good reception and feel that my monthly bill is reasonable. But what makes me take time out to write this is their customer service reps. Really friendly and helpful. I call often and I always feel like they care and really want to help me. I transferred my phone to T-Mobile and only lasted two days. Once the transfer was complete, I could not get mobile data, (internet access). All I had was phone and text access. I made more than fifteen phone calls to T-Mobile, visited two different stores and still got not satisfaction. I talked to customer service, two supervisors, technical support and engineering. I was passed around, put on hold and hung up on. I was told at the store they could not help me. Engineering said they would put in a work ticket and get back to me in one week. I use the phone to access the internet on my home computer and on the phone. A week without internet access???? A week without a response to my problem??Unacceptable. Customer service reps  were all from middle east and could hardly be understood. No one seemed to be least be concerned in helping me in a timely manner. I switched to another company and was back on line immediately. I highly recommend not using T-Mobile. Cheaper rates...yeah, but definitely get what you pay for.

I wish I could give 0 stars!  After 5 years of loyalty, WE ARE DONE! There customer service teams is trained to speak to the customers using a nice tone and pretending like they care to help yet they are not trained well enough to actually help. I have been a t-mobile customer for over 4 years now and moved to an area that I started having horrible service in. I work in an Emergency room and need dependable service which I was not getting and called many times. They act like they want to help yet nothing was done to help. My wife has the same issue on two of the exact same phones. The phone is an Lg nexus 5x which is a good smartphone with the latest android software. After not being help by there technical support, I was told to go to the tmobile store to get a new sim card. I did just that and the person working at the t mobile store was confused as to why they told me that because that did not make sense as a fix to my issue. He told me that Tmobile has a new technology from their towers and that my nexus 5x did not support that technology and the only way to get good service in my area would be to get a new phone with that new technology built in. To me this is fraud. I pay the same price any other tmobile customer pays and if my phone isnt good enough for there new technology to give me reliable service then they should tell me and I'll go to Verizon but instead they drag it out and offer no real solution all while continuing to take my money for extremely unreliable service. This is ridiculous and people should be aware of this practice.

I wish I can give (negative) -1milon.. So bad. If it was not for their plans and service added to the plans, I would take my 6 lines, now 4 and go somewhere ales in a heartbeat. ("TRUE" unlimited data, talk and txt, not fake - basically data without no borders any where).Back to customer server..:

Finally my luck I had a smart supervisor able to over-ride (i dont believe that) but than he ran my credit, OH BY THAN Christmas special over.
- Now in February 2018, I added 2 extra lines, total of 6. They will send me the sim by mail. (Big mistake), I got the sims, I needed to call them and associate these sims to the new lines. This extremly dumb guy, he switched my number sim while I end up lossing the sim that has to go to another number.. Wow. I called, another 20 min of waiting, 1 hour talking and getting another guys to re-enabe the old sim and than switch around back to the way it was, a night mare.
- Now beginning of March 2018, they have this reach out to customers to review if we are suttisfied. They call them selves "higher level of custome service". Oh My God.... She was a typical sales agent. I told her about some of these problems, and mentioning how I have wiered txt msgs that my data will be reduced and ending up when I call them back its not true, SHE end up telling me the rules and regulations of some service plans.. Wow, nothing to do about my problems, but rules and regulation of all their services that we have.
- HONESTLY, I am so tired of customer service, I am so afraid if anything goes wrong with any of my lines. I need to dedicate 1/2 half a day on fixing 1 particular problem. So incapable. If it was not for the T-Mobile global market (thank you Deutchland, and the price plan) seriously, I will rather get walkie talkies. So tired of them.
- Mar 23, - Supervisor of this team called me. Again, seems that they are not paying attention. SO FRUSTRATING WITH THIS service

5 years with T-Mobile. 3 years bad service. I had to fight for 5 months with them to get a true unlimited plan that was supposably grandfathered in. None of the count the MB usage that they advertise now. No signal in most places a phone that coincidentally slows down when my 2 year lease is up. I went to upgrade to the s9 from the s7.

S9 can only be financed. Cannot be paid in full at the store. Now they say that I can't change my phone because it has a scratch on the button. Hello! If my phone was brand new I wouldn't want to trade it in.

 They never call back after a customer service call and have a tendency to prove to normal citizens that because their a cooperate company customer gains or losses don't matter to them anymore.
